bullet_points: Load the three provided CSV files.
Convert weekly mortgage rates to monthly averages.
Merge all series into a single monthly dataframe covering the 1987-01 to 2024 period.
Print a summary of the merged dataframe (shape, date range, descriptive statistics) and verify no missing values.
Apply log-transformations to Prices and Starts.
Run Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) unit root tests on all variables before and after differencing; print test statistics and p-values.
Compute first differences for all variables to ensure stationarity and save the processed dataframe.
Specify the VAR model structure using the processed data.
Print AIC/BIC values for candidate lag lengths (1-12) and select the optimal lag.
Estimate the VAR model and perform diagnostic checks (Portmanteau test).
Implement the CCC-GARCH model on VAR residuals; if convergence fails, use Newey-West standard errors as a fallback.
Print convergence status and key GARCH parameter estimates.
Save the estimated model parameters and residuals.
Apply sign restrictions (Demand, Supply, Financing Cost shocks) using the Rubio-Ramirez algorithm.
Ensure at least 1000 accepted rotation matrices are drawn; print the acceptance rate and total count of accepted draws.
Compute IRFs and save the median responses along with 16th and 84th percentile confidence bands.
Save structural impact matrices.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Structural Identification and Impulse Response Analysis

bullet_points: Partition the data into Pre-2008 and Post-2012 regimes.
Re-estimate the model for each sub-sample.
Conduct formal structural break/stability tests and print the results.
Save sub-sample model parameters and stability statistics.
Perform counterfactual simulations for the 2020-2022 period (holding financing variables constant at 2019 levels).
Save the resulting counterfactual paths for Prices, Starts, and Inventory.
Generate a consolidated multi-panel figure for IRFs (showing responses with confidence bands).
Generate a consolidated multi-panel figure for sub-sample comparisons.
Generate a consolidated multi-panel figure for counterfactual simulation paths.
Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files.
Calculate and print FEVD tables for the housing price index at 1, 3, 6, 12, and 24-month horizons.
Generate and save a stacked bar chart representing the FEVD across these horizons.
Synthesize findings from IRFs, FEVD, sub-sample stability tests, and counterfactual simulations.
Interpret structural drivers (demand vs. supply vs. financing) and evolution of market dynamics.
Write the full Results section, explicitly referencing numerical results (FEVD percentages, IRF peaks, counterfactual deviations) from console outputs and saved statistics.
Structure the report with subsections for IRF analysis, sub-sample comparison, counterfactual simulation, and variance decomposition.
